state data.\n203\t\n204\t        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n205\t        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n206\t        \"\"\"\n207\t        import re\n208\t\n209\t        # Parse filter expression\n210\t        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(!=|>=|<=|>|<|==)(.+)', filter_expr)\n211\t        if not match:\n212\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid filter expression: {filter_expr}\")\n213\t            return True\n214\t\n215\t        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n216\t\n217\t        # Get field value from nested dict\n218\t        field_value = \"!=\":\n226\t                return str(field_value) != value\n227\t            elif op == \"==\":\n228\t                return str(field_value) == value\n229\t            elif op == \">\":\n230\t                return float(field_value) > float(value)\n231\t            elif op == \"<\":\n232\t                return float(field_value) < float(value)\n233\t            elif op == \">=\":\n234\t                return float(field_value) >= float(value)\n235\t            elif op == \"<=\":\n236\t                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n237\t        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n238\t            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n239\t            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n240\t            return        return False\n289\t\n290\t    async def push_monitoring_result(\n291\t        self,\n292\t        rule: MonitoringRule,\n293\t        current_state: dict,\n294\t        session_id: str,\n295\t      \"\"\"\n        executor = get_fetch_executor()\n\n        # Map intent_type to fetch_type\n        fetch_type = INTENT_TYPE_TO_FETCH.get(rule.intent_type, FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS)\n\n        #       timeout=rule.check_interval,\n        )\n\n        # Execute fetch\n        result = await executor.execute(command)\n\n        if result.success:\n            state_data = {\n                \"project_slug\": rule.project_slug,\n                \"intent_type\": rule.intent_type,\n                **result.data,\n            }\n\n            # Apply filters if specified\n            if rule.filters:\n                if not self._passes_filters(state_data, rule.filters):\n                    # Filter means we don't report this state\n                    logger.debug(f\"State for {rule.topic_id} filtered by {rule.filters}\")\n                    return None\n\n            return state_data\n        else:\n            logger.warning(f\"Fetch failed for {rule.topic_id}: {result.error}\")\n            return None\n\n    def _passes_filters(self, state_data: dict, filters: list[str]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if state data passes all filters.\n\n        Filters are simple expressions like \"phase!=Running\" or \"restarts>0\".\n        Returns False if any filter fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        for filter_expr in filters:\n            if not self._evaluate_filter(state_data, filter_expr):\n                return False\n        return True\n\n    def _evaluate_filter(self, state_data: dict, filter_expr: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a single filter expression against state data.\n\n        Supports: !=, >, <, >=, <=, ==\n        Examples: \"phase!=Running\", \"restarts>0\", \"ready!=1/1\"\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Parse filter expression\n        match = re.match(r'(\\w+)(!=|>=|<=|>|<|==)(.+)', filter_expr)\n        if not match:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid filter expression: {filter_expr}\")\n            return True\n\n        field_path, op, value = match.groups()\n\n        # Get field value from nested dict\n        field_value = self._get_nested_value(state_data, field_path)\n\n        # Compare\n        if field_value is None:\n            return False\n\n        try:\n            if op == \"!=\":\n                return str(field_value) != value\n            elif op == \"==\":\n                return str(field_value) == value\n            elif op == \">\":\n                return float(field_value) > float(value)\n            elif op == \"<\":\n                return float(field_value) < float(value)\n            elif op == \">=\":\n                return float(field_value) >= float(value)\n            elif op == \"<=\":\n                return float(field_value) <= float(value)\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            # If comparison fails, treat as no match\n            logger.warning(f\"Filter comparison failed: {filter_expr} with value {field_value}\")\n            return False\n\n        return True\n\n    def _get_nested_value(self, data: dict, path: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a value from nested dict using dot notation.\"\"\"\n        keys = path.split(\".\")\n        value = data\n        for key in keys:\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                value = value.get(key)\n            else:\n                return None\n        return value\n\n    async def detect_state_change(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Detect if the state has changed since last check.\n\n        Compares current state with last known state for the topic.\n        Returns True if significant change detected.\n        \"\"\"\n        topic_key = f\"{rule.project_slug}:{rule.intent_type}\"\n\n        if topic_key not in self.last_state:\n            # First check - store state and don't notify\n            self.last_state[topic_key] = current_state\n            return False\n\n        last_state = self.last_state[topic_key]\n\n        # Check for state changes based on notification threshold\n        if rule.notification_threshold == \"any_change\":\n            # Any field change triggers notification\n            return current_state != last_state\n        elif rule.notification_threshold == \"state_change\":\n            # Only notify if specific state fields change\n            # For status intents, check if phase, status, or health changed\n            state_fields = [\"phase\", \"status\", \"health\", \"ready\", \"sync_status\"]\n            return any(\n                current_state.get(field) != last_state.get(field)\n                for field in state_fields\n            )\n\n        return False\n\n    async def push_monitoring_result(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Push a monitoring result to the active surface.\n\n        Creates a result in the session store and pushes to appropriate surface.\n        \"\"\"\n        store = get_store()\n\n        # Find or create topic for this monitoring rule\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=rule.topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            topic_type=\"project\",\n            project_slugs=[rule.project_slug] if rule.project_slug else None,\n        )\n\n        # Create a monitoring intent\n        intent_id = await intent_type=rule.intent_type,\n        )\n\n        # Create result with diff info if available\n        topic_key = f\"{rule.project_slug}:{rule.intent_type}\"\n        previous_state = self.last_state.get(topic_key, {})\n\n        result_data = {\n            \"monitoring\": True,\n            \"current_state\": current_state,\n            \"previous_state\": previous_state if previous_state else None,\n            \"rule_filters\": rule.filters,\n        }\n\n        # Add diff if we have previous state\n        if previous_state:\n            result_data[\"diff\"] = self._compute_diff(previous_state, current_state)\n\n        result_id = await store.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=self._generate_summary(rule, current_state, previous_state),\n            data=result_data,\n            urgency=rule.urgency,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created monitoring result {result_id} for topic {rule.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Update last state\n        self.last_state[topic_key] = current_state\n\n    def _compute_diff(self, previous: dict, current: dict) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Compute diff between previous and current state.\"\"\"\n        diff = {}\n\n        for key in set(list(previous.keys()) + list(current.keys())):\n            prev_val = previous.get(key)\n            curr_val = current.get(key)\n\n            if prev_val != curr_val:\n                diff[key] = {\n                    \"from\": prev_val,\n                    \"to\": curr_val,\n                }\n\n        return diff\n\n    def _generate_summary(\n        self,\n        rule: MonitoringRule,\n        current_state: dict,\n        previous_state: Optional[dict],\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a summary for the monitoring result.\"\"\"\n        if previous_state is None:\n            return f\"Initial state check for {rule.topic_id}\"\n\n        # Check for specific state changes\n        if \"phase\" in current_state:\n            curr_phase = current_state[\"phase\"]\n            prev_phase = previous_state.get(\"phase\")\n            if curr_phase != prev_phase:\n                return f\"{rule.topic_id} phase changed from {prev_phase} to {curr_phase}\"\n\n        if \"sync_status\" in current_state:\n            curr_sync = current_state[\"sync_status\"]\n            prev_sync = previous_state.get(\"sync_status\")\n            if curr_sync != prev_sync:\n                return f\"{rule.topic_id} sync status changed from {prev_sync} to {curr_sync}\"\n\n        return f\"{rule.topic_id} state has changed\"\n\n    async def monitor_topic(self, rule: MonitoringRule) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Monitor a single topic on its check interval.\n\n        Runs in a loop until cancelled.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Starting monitor for topic {rule.topic_id} (interval: {rule.check_interval}s)\")\n\n        # Use a default session for monitoring results\n        # In full implementation, this would use the active session\n        session_id =     if current_state:\n                    # Detect change\n                    has_change = await self.detect_state_change(rule, current_state)\n\n                    if has_change:\n                        logger.info(f\"State change detected for {rule.topic_id}\")\n                        await self.push_monitoring_result(rule, current_state, session_id)\n                    else:\n \"\"\"Stop ambient monitoring.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Stopping ambient monitoring service\")\n        self.running = False\n\n        # Cancel all monitor tasks\n        for task in self.tasks:\n            task.cancel()\n\n        # Wait for tasks to complete\n        await asyncio.gather(*self.tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n        self.tasks.clear()\n\n        # Close HTTP client\n        if self._http_client:\n            await self._http_client.close()\n\n        logger.info(\"Ambient monitoring stopped\")\n\n    async def reload_config(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reload monitoring configuration.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Reloading monitoring configuration\")\n\n        old_tasks = self.tasks\n        self.tasks = []\n\n        # Cancel old monitors\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: CI/build status timestamps, deployment times.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                # Fetch CI status for timing info\n                ci_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.CI_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n   project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                deploy_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(deploy_cmd)\n                if deploy_result.success:\n                    if project_slug not in result:\n                        result[project_slug] = {}\n                    result[project_slug][\"deployment\"] = events.\"\"\"\n            # For now, return recent status that might explain why something happened\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"pods\": status_result.data.get(\"pods\", []),\n                        \"recent_events\": status_result.data.get(\"events\", []),\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_what_changed(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: git diff, recent commits, history.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                git_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(git_cmd)\n                if git_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = git_result.data\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_is_ready(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: health status, ready condition, completion status.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    pods = status_result.data.get(\"pods\", [])\n                    ready_count = sum(1 for p in pods if p.get(\"phase\") == \"Running\")\n                    result[project_slug] = {\n                        \"health\": \"healthy\" if ready_count == len(pods) and len(pods) > 0 else \"degraded\",\n                        \"ready\": ready_count == len(pods) and len(pods) > 0,\n                        \"completion\": f\"{ready_count}/{len(pods)}\" if pods else \"0/0\",\n                    }\n            return result\n\n        async def fetch_more_detail(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: extended metrics, detailed status.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                # Fetch comprehensive status\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    result[project_slug] = status_result.data\n\n                # Fetch ArgoCD status if fetch_status_check(topic_id: str, project_slugs: list[str]) -> dict:\n            \"\"\"Pre-fetch: current status, phase, health.\"\"\"\n            result = {}\n            for project_slug in project_slugs:\n                status_cmd = FetchCommand(\n                    fetch_type=FetchType.KUBECTL_STATUS,\n                    project_slug=project_slug,\n                    args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                status_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(status_cmd)\n                if status_result.success:\n                    pods = args=[],\n                    timeout=30,\n                )\n                ci_result = await self._fetch_executor.execute(ci_cmd)\n                if ci_result.success:\n confidence scores.\n        \"\"\"\n        predictions = []\n\n        # Detect patterns in utterance\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n\n        # HOW_LONG pattern: \"how long\", \"when\", \"time remaining\", \"ETA\"\n        if re.search(r\"how long|when will|time remaining|eta|estimated\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.HOW_LONG,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.8,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # WHY pattern: \"why\", \"how come\", \"reason\"\n        if re.search(r\"why|how come|reason for|what caused\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.WHY,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.7,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # WHAT_CHANGED pattern: \"what changed\", \"what's different\", \"since\"\n        if re.search(r\"what changed|what's different|what's new|since|from before\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.WHAT_CHANGED,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.75,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # IS_READY pattern: \"is it ready\", \"is it done\", \"finished\", \"complete\"\n        if re.search(r\"is it ready|is it done|finished|complete|ready yet\", utterance_lower):\n            predictions.append(PrefetchPrediction(\n                pattern=FollowUpPattern.IS_READY,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                project_slugs=project_slugs,\n                intent_type=intent_type,\n                confidence=0.85,\n                prefetch_data={},\n                created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                expires_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) + self.ttl_seconds,\n            ))\n\n        # Store utterance for context\n        self._store_utterance(session_id, utterance, topic_id, intent_type)\n\n        return predictions\n\n    def _store_utterance(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Store utterance for pattern analysis.\"\"\"\n        if session_id not in self._recent_utterances:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = []\n\n        self._recent_utterances[session_id].append({\n            \"utterance\": utterance,\n            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n            \"intent_type\": intent_type,\n            \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        })\n\n        # Keep only last 20 utterances\n        if len(self._recent_utterances[session_id]) > 20:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = self._recent_utterances[session_id][-20:]\n\n    async def prefetch_for_predictions(\n        self,\n        predictions: list[PrefetchPrediction],\n    ) -> dict[str, PrefetchCache]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute prefetch for a list of predictions.\n\n        Returns a dict of cache entries keyed by pattern.\n        \"\"\"\n        cache_entries = {}\n\n        for prediction in predictions:\n            # Skip low confidence predictions\n            if prediction.confidence < 0.5:\n                continue\n\n            # Check if we already have valid cache\n            cache_key = f\"{prediction.topic_id}:{prediction.pattern.value}\"\n            if cache_key in self._cache and self._cache[cache_key].is_valid():\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache hit for {cache_key}\")\n                cache_entries[cache_key] = self._cache[cache_key]\n                continue\n\n            # Fetch data\n            callback = self._fetch_callbacks.get(prediction.pattern)\n            if not callback:\n                logger.warning(f\"No fetch callback for pattern {prediction.pattern}\")\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                data = await callback(\n                    prediction.topic_id,\n                    prediction.project_slugs,\n                )\n\n                # Create cache entry\n                cache_entry = PrefetchCache(\n                    key=cache_key,\n                    data=data,\n                    created_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    expires_at=prediction.expires_at,\n                )\n\n                self._cache[cache_key] = cache_entry\n                cache_entries[cache_key] = cache_entry\n\n                logger.info(f\"Prefetched data for {cache_key}\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Prefetch error for {prediction.pattern}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n        return cache_entries\n\n    def get_prefetch_data(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        pattern: FollowUpPattern,\n    ) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get pre-fetched data for a topic and pattern.\n\n        Returns None if no valid cache exists.\n        \"\"\"\n        cache_key = f\"{topic_id}:{pattern.value}\"\n\n        if cache_key not in self._cache:\n            return None\n\n        cache_entry = self._cache[cache_key]\n\n        if not cache_entry.is_valid():\n            # Cache expired, remove it\n            del self._cache[cache_key]\n            return None\n\n        # Record cache hit\n        cache_entry.record_hit()\n\n        return cache_entry.data\n\n    def get_all_prefetch_data(self, topic_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pre-fetched data for a topic.\"\"\"\n        result = {}\n        prefix = f\"{topic_id}:\"\n\n        for key, cache_entry in self._cache.items():\n            if key.startswith(prefix) and cache_entry.is_valid():\n                pattern = key[len(prefix):]\n                result[pattern] = {\n                    \"data\": cache_entry.data,\n                    \"hit_count\": cache_entry.hit_count,\n                    \"expires_at\": cache_entry.expires_at,\n                }\n\n        return result\n\n    async def cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Clean up expired cache entries. Returns count of cleaned entries.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        expired_keys = [\n            key for key, cache in self._cache.items()\n            if cache.expires_at < now\n        ]\n\n        for key in expired_keys:\n            del self._cache[key]\n\n        if expired_keys:\n            logger.info(f\"Cleaned up {len(expired_keys)} expired prefetch entries\")\n\n        return len(expired_keys)\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get prefetch statistics.\"\"\"\n        total_hits = sum(cache.hit_count for cache in self._cache.values())\n\n        return {\n            \"cache_size\": len(self._cache),\n            \"total_hits\": total_hits,\n            \"valid_entries\": sum(1 for cache in self._cache.values() if cache.is_valid()),\n            \"expired_entries\": sum(1 for cache in self._cache.values() if not cache.is_valid()),\n        }\n\n    def clear_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear utterance history for a session.\"\"\"\n        if session_id in self._recent_utterances:\n            del self._recent_utterances[session_id]\n\n\n# Global prefetcher instance\n_prefetcher: Optional[SpeculativePrefetcher] = None\n\n\ndef get_prefetcher(ttl_seconds: int = 300) -> SpeculativePrefetcher:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global speculative prefetcher instance.\"\"\"\n    global _prefetcher\n    if _prefetcher is None:\n        _prefetcher = SpeculativePrefetcher(ttl_seconds=ttl_seconds)\n    return _prefetcher\n","numLines":497,"startLine":1,"totalLines":497}}}

async def run(self) -> None:\n391\t        \"\"\"Main loop: periodically analyze signals and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n392\t        logger.info(f\"Starting background analysis processor (interval: analysis processor for implicit feedback signals.\n\nReads feedback signals from the session store and proposes artifact updates\nas canvas cards for the self-modification agent to process.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..agents.self_modification import SelfModificationAgent, get_self_modification_agent\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass AnalysisTrigger(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Triggers for background analysis.\"\"\"\n    SIGNAL_COUNT_THRESHOLD = \"signal_count_threshold\"  # Process after N signals\n    TIME_INTERVAL = \"time_interval\"  # Process every N seconds\n    HIGH_URGENCY_SIGNAL = \"high_urgency_signal\"  # Process immediately for critical signals\n    MANUAL = \"manual\"  # Triggered by user command\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AnalysisProposal:\n    \"\"\"A proposal for artifact update based on signal analysis.\"\"\"\n    proposal_id: str\n    signal_type: str\n    artifact_type: str  # 'prompt', 'config', 'component'\n    artifact_name: str\n    change_summary: str\n    confidence: float\n    signals_consulted: int\n    generated_at: int\n    data: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_canvas_card(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to canvas card format for surfacing to user.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"id\": self.proposal_id,\n            \"type\": \"artifact_update_proposal\",\n            \"title\": f\"Update {self.artifact_type}: {self.artifact_name}\",\n            \"summary\": self.change_summary,\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\" if self.confidence < 0.8 else \"high\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"artifact_type\": self.artifact_type,\n                \"artifact_name\": self.artifact_name,\n                \"change_summary\": self.change_summary,\n                \"confidence\": self.confidence,\n                \"signals_consulted\": self.signals_consulted,\n            },\n            \"actions\": [\n                {\"id\": \"approve\", \"label\": \"Approve\", \"type\": \"primary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"reject\", \"label\": \"Reject\", \"type\": \"secondary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"defer\", \"label\": \"Defer\", \"type\": \"tertiary\"},\n            ],\n            \"created_at\": self.generated_at,\n        }\n\n\nclass BackgroundAnalysisProcessor:\n    \"\"\"\n    Processes implicit feedback signals and proposes artifact updates.\n\n    Runs as a background task that:\n    1. Reads unprocessed feedback signals from the session store\n    2. Analyzes patterns in the signals\n    3. Proposes artifact updates via self-modification agent\n    4. Surfaces proposals as canvas cards\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        signal_threshold: int = 10,\n        check_interval: int = 60,\n    ):\n        self.signal_threshold = signal_threshold\n        self.check_interval = check_interval\n        self.running = False\n        self.task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        self.store = get_store()\n        self.self_mod_agent = get_self_modification_agent()\n\n    async def analyze_signals(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze unprocessed feedback signals and generate proposals.\n\n        Args:\n            limit: Maximum number of signals to process in one batch\n\n        Returns:\n            List of proposals for artifact updates\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get unprocessed signals\n        signals = await self.store.get_unprocessed_signals(limit=limit)\n\n        if not signals:\n            logger.debug(\"No unprocessed signals to analyze\")\n            return []\n\n        logger.info(f\"Analyzing {len(signals)} feedback signals\")\n\n        # Group signals by type\n        signals_by_type = self._group_by_type(signals)\n\n        # Generate proposals for each signal type\n        proposals = []\n\n        for signal_type, type_signals in signals_by_type.items():\n            type_proposals = await self._analyze_signal_type(signal_type, type_signals)\n            proposals.extend(type_proposals)\n\n        # Mark processed signals\n        signal_ids = [s[\"id\"] for s in signals]\n        await self.store.mark_signals_processed(signal_ids)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Generated {len(proposals)} proposals from {len(signals)} signals\")\n\n        return proposals\n\n    def _group_by_type(self, signals: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:\n        \"\"\"Group signals by type.\"\"\"\n        grouped = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            signal_type = signal[\"signal_type\"]\n            if signal_type not in grouped:\n                grouped[signal_type] = []\n            grouped[signal_type].append(signal)\n        return grouped\n\n    async def _analyze_signal_type(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str,\n        signals: list[dict],\n    ) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"Analyze signals of a specific type and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        if signal_type == \"ack_speed\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_ack_speed(signals))\n        elif signal_type == \"follow_up_pattern\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_follow_up_patterns(signals))\n        elif signal_type == \"surface_switch\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_surface_switches(signals))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_requery\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_requeries(signals))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_skipped\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_skipped_results(signals))\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_continuation\":\n            # Topic continuation signals are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_switch\":\n            # Topic switches are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_ack_speed(self, signals: list[dict]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze acknowledgment speed signals.\n\n        Slow acks may indicate:\n        - Poor narration quality (for audio)\n        - Unclear result formatting\n        - Wrong delivery surface\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Calculate average ack delay\n        delays = []\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n            delays.append(delay)\n\n        if not delays:\n            return proposals\n\n        avg_delay = sum(delays) / len(delays)\n\n        # If average ack delay is high, propose improvements\n        if avg_delay > 60:  # More than 1 minute average\n            # Check which surface type has the slowest acks\n            surface_delays = {}\n            for signal in signals:\n                data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n                surface = data.get(\"surface_type\", \"unknown\")\n                delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n                if surface not in surface_delays:\n                    surface_delays[surface] = []\n                surface_delays[surface].append(delay)\n\n            # Find worst surface\n            worst_surface = None\n            worst_avg = 0\n            for surface, s_delays in surface_delays.items():\n                s_avg = sum(s_delays) / len(s_delays)\n                if s_avg > worst_avg:\n                    worst_avg = s_avg\n                    worst_surface = surface\n\n            if worst_surface == \"audio\":\n                # Propose improving narration prompts\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s (target: <30s). Consider shorter, more concise summaries.\",\n                    confidence=0.7,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n            elif worst_surface == \"canvas\":\n                # Propose improving result formatting\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"component\",\n                    artifact_name=\"result_card\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result card formatting: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s. Consider more prominent summaries and clearer action buttons.\",\n                    confidence=0.65,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_follow_up_patterns(self, signals: list[dict]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze follow-up pattern signals.\n\n        Follow-up patterns indicate what was missing from previous results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count follow-up types\n        follow_up_types = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            f_type = data.get(\"follow_up_type\", \"unknown\")\n            if f_type not in follow_up_types:\n                follow_up_types[f_type] = 0\n            follow_up_types[f_type] += 1\n\n        # Generate proposals for common follow-up types\n        for f_type, count in follow_up_types.items():\n            if count >= 3:  # Need at least 3 similar follow-ups to propose change\n                if f_type == \"why_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_explainatory\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add more 'why' explanations to results. {count} users asked 'why' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.8,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"temporal_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_timing\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add timing information to results. {count} users asked 'how long/when' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.75,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"more_detail\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_verbose\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Provide more detailed results by default. {count} users asked for more detail recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.7,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_surface_switches(self, signals: list[dict]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze surface switch signals.\n\n        Audio-to-canvas switches with pending results indicate poor narration.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count audio-to-canvas switches with pending results\n        audio_to_canvas_count = 0\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            if data.get(\"reason\") == \"audio_to_canvas_with_pending\":\n                audio_to_canvas_count += 1\n\n        if audio_to_canvas_count >= 5:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"surface_switch\",\n                artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration quality. {audio_to_canvas_count} users switched to canvas with pending results, indicating narration was insufficient.\",\n                confidence=0.85,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                data={\"audio_to_canvas_count\": audio_to_canvas_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_requeries(self, signals: list[dict]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze re-query signals.\n\n        Re-queries indicate inadequate results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Group by utterance to find frequently re-queried items\n        requeries = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            utterance = data.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n            attempt_count = data.get(\"attempt_count\", 0)\n            if utterance and attempt_count > 1:\n                if utterance not in requeries:\n                    requeries[utterance] = 0\n                requeries[utterance] = max(requeries[utterance], attempt_count)\n\n        # Find queries with 3+ attempts\n        for utterance, max_attempts in requeries.items():\n            if max_attempts >= 3:\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"result_requery\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"synthesize_completeness\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result completeness for query type: '{utterance[:50]}...'. Users re-queried {max_attempts} times.\",\n                    confidence=0.75,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    data={\"utterance\": utterance, \"max_attempts\": max_attempts},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_skipped_results(self, signals: list[dict]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze skipped result signals.\n\n        Skipped results indicate low interest or poor delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count skipped results\n        skipped_count = len(signals)\n\n        if skipped_count >= 10:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"result_skipped\",\n                artifact_type=\"config\",\n                artifact_name=\"monitoring\",\n                change_summary=f\"Reduce monitoring verbosity. {skipped_count} results were skipped (not acknowledged) before surface disconnect.\",\n                confidence=0.6,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                data={\"skipped_count\": skipped_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main loop: periodically analyze signals and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Starting background analysis processor (interval: {self.check_interval}s)\")\n\n        while self.running:\n            try:\n                # Analyze signals\n                proposals = await self.analyze_signals()\n\n                if proposals:\n                    # Surface proposals as canvas cards\n                    for proposal in proposals:\n                        card = proposal.to_canvas_card()\n                        # TODO: Send to canvas via SSE\n                        logger.info(f\"Generated proposal: {proposal.change_summary}\")\n\n                # Wait for next cycle\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in background analysis loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval)\n\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n        if self.task is not None and not self.task.done():\n            logger.warning(\"Background analysis processor already running\")\n            return\n\n        self.running = True\n        self.task = asyncio.create_task(self.run())\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n        self.running = False\n        if self.task:\n            self.task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self.task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n\n\n# Global background analysis processor instance\n_background_processor: Optional[BackgroundAnalysisProcessor] = None\n\n\ndef get_background_processor(\n    signal_threshold: int = 10,\n    check_interval: int = 60,\n) -> BackgroundAnalysisProcessor:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global background analysis processor instance.\"\"\"\n    global _background_processor\n    if _background_processor is None:\n        _background_processor = BackgroundAnalysisProcessor(\n            signal_threshold=signal_threshold,\n            check_interval=check_interval,\n        )\n    return _background_processor\n","numLines":455,"startLine":1,"totalLines":455}}}

dispatch_intent(\n    utterance: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    voice_session: Any,  # VoiceSession instance\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch an utterance to the intent router.\n\n    Tool-as-trigger: returns ack immediately, results arrive async.\n\n    Phase 3 enhancements:\n    - Detects follow-up questions via conversation tracker\n    - Triggers prefetch for likely follow-up patterns\n    - Uses pre-fetched data when available\n    - Tracks implicit feedback signals\n    \"\"\"\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Get Phase 3 services\n    conversation_tracker = get_conversation_tracker()\n    prefetcher = get_prefetcher()\n\n    # Detect if this is a follow-up question\n    detected_topics = []  # Would for pre-fetched data if this is a follow-up\n    prefetch_data = None\n    if is_follow_up and suggested_topic_id:\n        # Check if we have pre-fetched data for likely patterns\n        prefetch_data = prefetcher.get_all_prefetch_data(suggested_topic_id)\n        if prefetch_data:\n            logger.info(f\"Found pre-fetched data for topic {suggested_topic_id}: {list(prefetch_data.keys())}\")\n\n    # Store utterance\n    store = get_store()\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance)\n\n    # Call router (this will spawn fetch+synthesize workers)\n    try:\n        router_request = {\n            \"utterance\": utterance,\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n        }\n\n        # Include pre-fetched data if available (Phase 3)\n        if prefetch_data:\n            router_request[\"prefetch_data\"] = prefetch_data\n            router_request[\"is_follow_up\"] = ROUTER_API_URL,\n                json=router_request,\n            )\n            resp.raise_for_status()\n            router_result = resp.json()\n\n        # Log the routing result\n        logger.info(f\"Router result: {json.dumps(router_result, indent=2)}\")\n\n        # The router has dispatched intents to workers\n        # Results will arrive via result queue and be narrated\n        # Return ack for the tool call\n        intent_count = len(router_result.get(\"intents\", []))\n\n        # Track conversation turn\n        await conversation_tracker.record_turn(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            utterance=utterance,\n            primary_topic_id=suggested_topic_id if is_follow_up else None,\n            is_follow_up=is_follow_up,\n        )\n\n        # Trigger prefetch for likely follow-up patterns\n        if suggested_topic_id:\n            # Get project slugs for the topic (simplified - in practice, would fetch from topic)\n      )\n                # Prefetch for high-confidence predictions\n                if predictions:\n                    await prefetcher.prefetch_for_predictions(predictions)\n\n        # Return acknowledgment for the tool call\n        if intent_count == 0:\n            return \"I'm not sure what you're asking about. Could you clarify?\"\n        elif intent_count == 1:\n            return \"On it.\"\n        else:\n            return f\"Working on {intent_count} things.\"\n\n    except httpx.ConnectError:\n        logger.error(\"Router not available\")\n        return \"I'm having trouble routing that request right now.\"\n    except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\")\n        return \"Something went wrong with routing.\"\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return \"I'm having trouble with that request.\"\n\n\nasync def result_listener(\n    session_id: str,\n    voice_session: Any,\n    audio_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    use_batching: bool = True,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Background task that listens for async results and pushes them to the voice session.\n\n    Results arrive from the synthesize strand via SSE or direct call.\n    This task polls the session store for new results and pushes them.\n\n    Phase 3 enhancements:\n    - Computes diffs between consecutive results using diff engine\n    - Includes diff data in result payload for canvas rendering\n    - Tracks implicit feedback signals (ack speed, surface switches)\n\n    If use_batching is True (default for audio mode), results go through the\n    ResultBatcher which applies urgency-based batching rules.\n    Otherwise, results are pushed immediately.\n\n    Tracks successfully pushed result IDs individually to avoid duplicates\n    even if some pushes fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .continuity import push_to_canvas\n    from .batching import get_result_batcher\n    from ..feedback.signals import get_feedback_tracker\n\n    store = get_store()\n    batcher = get_result_batcher() if use_batching else None\n    diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n    feedback_tracker = get_feedback_tracker()\n    pushed_ids = set()  # Track successfully pushed result IDs in this session\n\n    # Track previous results per topic for diff computation\n    previous_results: dict[str, dict] = {}\n\n    # Set up batcher callback if using batching\n    if batcher:\n        async def narrate_batch(results: list) -> None:\n            \"\"\"Callback to narrate a batch of results.\"\"\"\n            for r in results:\n                try:\n                    result_data = {\n                        \"intent_id\": r.intent_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": r.topic_id,\n                        \"summary\": r.summary,\n                        \"urgency\": r.urgency.value,\n                        \"data\": r.data,\n                    }\n                    await voice_session.push_result(result_data)\n                    await push_to_canvas(session_id, result_data, audio_surface_id)\n                    logger.info(f\"Narrated result {r.result_id} (batched)\")\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to narrate result {r.result_id}: {e}\")\n\n        batcher.set_narrate_callback(narrate_batch)\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            # Check for new results\n            unsurfed = await store.get_unsurfaced_results(session_id)\n\n            newly_pushed = []\n            for result in unsurfed:\n                result_id = result[\"id\"]\n\n                # Skip if already pushed (e.g., from previous iteration after error)\n                if result_id in pushed_ids:\n                    continue\n\n                try:\n                    topic_id = result.get(\"topic_id\")\n                    result_data = {\n                        \"intent_id\": result[\"intent_id\"],\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"summary\": result[\"summary\"],\n                        \"urgency\": result[\"urgency\"],\n                        \"data\": json.loads(result[\"data\"]),\n                    }\n\n                    # Compute diff with previous result for this topic (Phase 3)\n                    if topic_id and topic_id in previous_results:\n                        previous_result = previous_results[topic_id]\n                        diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                            topic_id=topic_id,\n                            previous_result=previous_result,\n                            current_result=result,\n                        )\n                        # Include diff in result data\n                        if diff_result.has_changes:\n                            result_data[\"diff\"] = {\n                                \"has_changes\": True,\n                                \"change_summary\": diff_result.change_summary,\n                                \"fields\": [\n                                    {\n                                        \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                                        \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                                        \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                                        \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                                    }\n                                    for f in diff_result.fields\n                                ],\n                            }\n                            logger.info(f\"Computed diff for result {result_id}: {diff_result.change_summary}\")\n\n                    # Track previous result for diff computation\n                    if topic_id:\n                        previous_results[topic_id] = result\n\n                    # Track implicit feedback: result created\n                    await feedback_tracker.track_result_created(\n                        result_id=result_id,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        topic_id=topic_id,\n                    )\n\n                    if use_batching and batcher:\n                        # Queue with batcher for urgency-based narration\n                        await batcher.queue_result(\n                            result_id=result[\"id\"],\n                            intent_id=result[\"intent_id\"],\n                            topic_id=result[\"topic_id\"],\n                            summary=result[\"summary\"],\n                            data=json.loads(result[\"data\"]),\n                            urgency=result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                        )\n                        # Push to canvas immediately (canvas doesn't use batching)\n                        await push_to_canvas(session_id, result_data, audio_surface_id)\n                        logger.info(f\"Queued result {result_id} for batched audio narration\")\n                    else:\n                        # Push directly to voice session (no batching)\n                        await voice_session.push_result(result_data)\n                        await push_to_canvas(session_id, result_data, audio_surface_id)\n\n                    pushed_ids.add(result_id)\n                    newly_pushed.append(result_id)\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    # Failed to push this result - will retry on next iteration\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to push result {result_id}: {e}\")\n\n            if newly_pushed:\n                # Mark successfully pushed results as surfaced\n                await store.mark_results_surfed_by_ids(session_id, newly_pushed)\n\n            # Wait before next check\n            await asyncio.sleep(0.5)\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            break\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Result listener error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n","numLines":295,"startLine":1,"totalLines":295}}}

               # Quiet period within same day\n                return self.start <= current_time < self.end\n\n        except ImportError:\n            # Fallback if zoneinfo/pytz not available\n            logger.warning(\"zoneinfo/pytz not available, quiet hours disabled\")\n            return False\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass BatchingConfig:\n    \"\"\"Batching configuration.\"\"\"\n    # Batch window durations (seconds)\n    low_urgency_batch_seconds: int = 300  # 5 minutes\n    normal_urgency_batch_seconds: int = 120  # 2 minutes\n    high_urgency_batch_seconds: int = 0  # No batching\n    critical_urgency_batch_seconds: int = 0  # No batching\n\n    # Maximum batch size\n    max_batch_size: int = 10\n\n    # Quiet hours\n    quiet_hours: QuietHours = field(default_factory=QuietHours)\n\n\nclass ResultBatcher:\n    \"\"\"\n    Batches results for audio narration based on urgency and quiet hours.\n\n    Rules:\n    - Critical: Interrupt immediately\n    - High: Wait for natural pause\n    - Normal: Batch within ~5s window (configurable)\n    - Low: Narrate only if idle\n    - Respect quiet hours (non-critical suppressed)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.config = BatchingConfig()\n        self._pending: list[PendingResult] = []\n        self._waiting_for_pause: list[PendingResult] = []\n        self._last_narration: int = 0\n        self._session_active: bool = True\n        self._on_narrate_callback: Optional[Callable[[list[PendingResult]], Any]] = None\n        self._batch_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        self._running: bool = False\n\n        if config_path and config_path.exists():\n            self._load_config(config_path)\n\n    def _load_config(self, config_path: Path) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Load batching configuration from monitoring.yaml.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(config_path, \"r\") as f:\n                data = yaml.safe_load(f)\n\n            batching_config = data.get(\"batching\", {})\n            quiet_hours_config = data.get(\"quiet_hours\", {})\n\n            self.config.low_urgency_batch_seconds = batching_config.get(\n                \"low_urgency_batch_seconds\", 300\n            )\n            self.config.normal_urgency_batch_seconds = batching_config.get(\n                \"normal_urgency_batch_seconds\", 120\n            )\n\n            if quiet_hours_config:\n                self.config.quiet_hours = QuietHours(\n                    enabled=quiet_hours_config.get(\"enabled\", False),\n                    start=quiet_hours_config.get(\"start\", \"22:00\"),\n                    end=quiet_hours_config.get(\"end\", \"08:00\"),\n                    timezone=quiet_hours_config.get(\"timezone\", \"America/New_York\"),\n                    allow_critical=quiet_hours_config.get(\"allow_critical\", True),\n                )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Loaded batching config from {config_path}\")\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load batching config: {e}\")\n\n    def set_narrate_callback(self, callback: Callable[[list[PendingResult]], Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the callback to invoke when results should be narrated.\"\"\"\n        self._on_narrate_callback = callback\n\n    async def queue_result(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Queue a result for narration.\n\n        Returns immediately; narration happens per batching rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        urgency_enum = Urgency(urgency.lower())\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        pending = PendingResult(\n            result_id=result_id,\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency_enum,\n            created_at=now,\n        )\n\n        # Check quiet hours\n        if self.config.quiet_hours.is_quiet():\n            if urgency_enum != Urgency.CRITICAL or not self.config.quiet_hours.allow_critical:\n                logger.info(f\"Result {result_id} suppressed during quiet hours\")\n                return\n\n        # Handle based on urgency\n        if urgency_enum == Urgency.CRITICAL:\n            # Critical: interrupt immediately\n            await self._narrate_now([pending])\n\n        elif urgency_enum == Urgency.HIGH:\n            # High: wait for natural pause (or timeout)\n            self._waiting_for_pause.append(pending)\n            # Set timeout for high urgency (30s max wait)\n            asyncio.create_task(self._high_urgency_timeout(pending))\n\n        elif urgency_enum == Urgency.NORMAL:\n            # Normal: batch within window\n            self._pending.append(pending)\n            # Schedule batch if not already scheduled\n            if self._batch_task is None or self._batch_task.done():\n                self._batch_task = asyncio.create_task(\n                    self._normal_batch_timer(pending)\n                )\n\n        elif urgency_enum == Urgency.LOW:\n            # Low: only narrate if idle (no active conversation)\n            if self._session_active:\n                # Session is active, queue for idle narration\n                self._pending.append(pending)\n            else:\n                # Session idle, narrate immediately\n                await self._narrate_now([pending])\n\n    async def _narrate_now(self, results: list[PendingResult]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Narrate results immediately.\"\"\"\n        if not results:\n            return\n\n        self._last_narration = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        if self._on_narrate_callback:\n            try:\n                await self._on_narrate_callback(results)\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in narrate callback: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    async def _high_urgency_timeout(self, pending: PendingResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Timeout for high urgency results that haven't found a pause.\"\"\"\n        await asyncio.sleep(30)  # 30 second max wait\n        if pending in self._waiting_for_pause:\n            self._waiting_for_pause.remove(pending)\n            await self._narrate_now([pending])\n\n    async def _normal_batch_timer(self, pending: PendingResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Timer for normal urgency batch window.\"\"\"\n        await asyncio.sleep(self.config.normal_urgency_batch_seconds)\n\n        # Collect all normal urgency results that are ready\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        ready = [r for r in self._pending if now - r.created_at >= self.config.normal_urgency_batch_seconds]\n\n        # Remove from pending\n        for r in ready:\n            if r in self._pending:\n                self._pending.remove(r)\n\n        # Narrate batch\n        await self._narrate_now(ready)\n\n    async def signal_pause(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Signal a natural pause in conversation.\n\n        High urgency results waiting for pause can be narrated now.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._waiting_for_pause:\n            results = self._waiting_for_pause.copy()\n            self._waiting_for_pause.clear()\n            await self._narrate_now(results)\n\n    async def signal_idle(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Signal session is idle (no active conversation).\n\n        All pending results (including low urgency) can be narrated.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._session_active = False\n\n        if self._pending:\n            results = self._pending.copy()\n            self._pending.clear()\n            await self._narrate_now(results)\n\n    async def signal_active(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Signal session is active (conversation happening).\"\"\"\n        self._session_active = True\n\n    async def flush(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Flush all pending results immediately.\"\"\"\n        all_pending = self._waiting_for_pause + self._pending\n\n        if all_pending:\n            self._waiting_for_pause.clear()\n            self._pending.clear()\n            await self._narrate_now(all_pending)\n\n    def get_queue_status(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get current queue status for monitoring.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"pending\": len(self._pending),\n            \"waiting_for_pause\": len(self._waiting_for_pause),\n            \"session_active\": self._session_active,\n            \"last_narration\": self._last_narration,\n            \"quiet_hours_active\": self.config.quiet_hours.is_quiet(),\n        }\n\n\n# Global batcher instance\n_batcher: Optional[ResultBatcher] = None\n\n\ndef get_result_batcher(config_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> ResultBatcher:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global result batcher instance.\"\"\"\n    global _batcher\n    if _batcher is None:\n        config = config_path or Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/config/monitoring.yaml\")\n        _batcher = ResultBatcher(config_path=config)\n    return _batcher\n","numLines":310,"startLine":1,"totalLines":310}}}

            topic_id=topic_id,\n756\t                previous_result=previous_result,\n757\t                current_result={\"data\": data},\n758\t            )\n759\t\n760\t            if          ],\n773\t                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n774\t                }\n775\t\n776\t        # Create the result with diff data\n777\t        result_id = await
