store implementation using SQLite with WAL mode.\n\nStores sessions, surfaces, utterances, intents, results, topics, and intent_topics.\nProvides concurrent read access with serialized writes via WAL mode.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport aiosqlite\nimport sqlite3\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nimport httpx\n\n# Schema definition\nSCHEMA_SQL = \"\"\"\n-- Sessions: surface-agnostic persistent entities\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    primary_surface_id TEXT\n);\n\n-- Surfaces: transient windows into sessions\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS surfaces (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('canvas', 'telegram', 'audio')),\n    state           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(state IN ('active', 'idle', 'disconnected')) DEFAULT 'active',\n    always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(always_available IN (0, 1)),\n    last_seen       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n\n-- Utterances: raw input from user\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n\n-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    project_slug TEXT,\n    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n    bead_ref     TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_session ON intents(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_topic ON intents(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_status ON intents(status);\n\n-- Results: structured data returned by agents\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS results (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    intent_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n    data        TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON\n    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n\n-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n    session_id   TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    archived_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_session ON topics(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_scope ON topics(scope);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_active ON topics(last_active DESC);\n\n-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n\n-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    result_id    TEXT,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n    surface_type TEXT,\n    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n    processed_at INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\"\"\"\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Surface operations\n    async def register_surface(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n        always_available: bool = False,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        surface_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO surfaces\n                   (id, session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (surfaced after last surface disconnect)\n            # For now, return count of results in last hour\n            one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND created_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, one_hour_ago)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n                    surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            db_path = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\")\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":804,"startLine":1,"totalLines":804}}}

__init__(self):\n        # session_id -> TopicFocus\n        self._focus: dict[str, TopicFocus] = {}\n        # session_id -> list[ConversationTurn]\n        self._history: dict[str, self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        primary_topic_id: Optional[str],\n        related_topic_ids: list[str] = None,\n        is_follow_up: bool = False,\n    ) -> ConversationTurn:\n        \"\"\"\n        Record a conversation turn.\n\n        Updates the current focus and history for the session.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        turn_id = str(uuid4())\n\n        if related_topic_ids is None:\n            related_topic_ids = []\n\n        turn = ConversationTurn(\n            turn_id=turn_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            utterance=utterance,\n            primary_topic_id=primary_topic_id,\n            related_topic_ids=related_topic_ids,\n            timestamp=now,\n            was_follow_up=is_follow_up,\n        )\n\n        # Update focus\n        if primary_topic_id:\n            if session_id not in self._focus or self._focus[session_id].primary_topic_id != primary_topic_id:\n                # New or changed focus\n                self._focus[session_id] = TopicFocus(\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    primary_topic_id=primary_topic_id,\n                    primary_topic_label=await self._get_topic_label(primary_topic_id),\n                    last_activity=now,\n                    turn_count=1,\n                )\n            else:\n                # Same focus, update activity\n                self._focus[session_id].last_activity = now\n                self._focus[session_id].turn_count += 1\n\n        # Add to history\n        if session_id not in self._history:\n            self._history[session_id] = []\n        self._history[session_id].append(turn)\n\n        # Keep only last 20 turns\n        if len(self._history[session_id]) > 20:\n            self._history[session_id] = self._history[session_id][-20:]\n\n        # Update recent topics\n        self._update_recent_topics(session_id, primary_topic_id, related_topic_ids)\n\n        logger.debug(\n            f\"Recorded turn {turn_id} for session {session_id}, \"\n            f\"topic: {primary_topic_id}, follow_up: {is_follow_up}\"\n        )\n\n        return turn\n\n    async def detect_follow_up(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        detected_topics: list[str],\n    ) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Detect if an utterance is a follow-up to the current topic focus.\n\n        Returns (is_follow_up, suggested_topic_id).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if we have an active focus\n        focus = self._focus.get(session_id)\n        if not focus or not focus.is_valid():\n            return False, None\n\n        # Check if utterance contains follow-up indicators\n        follow_up_indicators = [\n            \"why\", \"how long\", \"since when\", \"what about\", \"and\", \"also\",\n            \"more detail\", \"tell me more\", \"what else\", \"then what\",\n        ]\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n        has_indicator = any(ind in utterance_lower for ind in follow_up_indicators)\n\n        # Check if detected topics include the focus topic\n        if focus.primary_topic_id in detected_topics:\n            # The utterance relates to the current focus topic\n            return True, focus.primary_topic_id\n\n        # Check if utterance is very short (likely follow-up)\n        # and no other topics detected\n        if len(utterance.split()) < 5 and len(detected_topics) == 0:\n            # Assume follow-up to current focus\n            return True, focus.primary_topic_id\n\n        # Check for pronouns that refer to \"it\", \"that\", \"this\"\n        pronoun_indicators = [\"it\", \"that\", \"this\", \"there\"]\n        if any(pronoun in utterance_lower.split() for pronoun in pronoun_indicators):\n            if has_indicator or len(detected_topics) == 0:\n                return True, focus.primary_topic_id\n\n        # Not a clear follow-up\n        return False, None\n\n    def get_focus(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[TopicFocus]:\n        \"\"\"Get the current topic focus for a session.\"\"\"\n        focus = self._focus.get(session_id)\n        if focus and focus.is_valid():\n            return focus\n        return None\n\n    def get_history(self, session_id: str, limit: int = 10) -> list[ConversationTurn]:\n        \"\"\"Get recent conversation turns for a session.\"\"\"\n        history = self._history.get(session_id, [])\n        return history[-limit:] if history else []\n\n    def _update_recent_topics(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        primary_topic_id: Optional[str],\n        related_topic_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update the list of recent topics for a session.\"\"\"\n        if session_id not in self._recent_topics:\n            self._recent_topics[session_id] = []\n\n        # Add primary topic\n        if primary_topic_id:\n            if primary_topic_id not in self._recent_topics[session_id]:\n                self._recent_topics[session_id].insert(0, primary_topic_id)\n\n        # Add related topics\n        for topic_id in related_topic_ids:\n            if topic_id not in self._recent_topics[session_id]:\n                self._recent_topics[session_id].append(topic_id)\n\n        # Keep only last 10\n        self._recent_topics[session_id] = self._recent_topics[session_id][:10]\n\n    async def _get_topic_label(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get the label for a topic.\"\"\"\n        store = get_store()\n        # This is a simplified lookup - in practice we'd query the topics table\n        # For now, return None\n        return None\n\n    def disambiguate(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        detected_topics: list[str],\n    ) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Disambiguate an utterance by applying conversation context.\n\n        Returns the most likely topic_id, or None if still ambiguous.\n        \"\"\"\n        # If only one topic detected, use it\n        if len(detected_topics) == 1:\n            return detected_topics[0]\n\n        # If no topics detected, check current focus\n        if not detected_topics:\n            focus = self.get_focus(session_id)\n            if focus:\n                return focus.primary_topic_id\n            return None\n\n        # Multiple topics detected - prefer current focus if it's in the list\n        focus = self.get_focus(session_id)\n        if focus and focus.primary_topic_id in detected_topics:\n            return focus.primary_topic_id\n\n        # Prefer most recently mentioned topic\n        recent = self._recent_topics.get(session_id, [])\n        for topic_id in recent:\n            if topic_id in detected_topics:\n                return topic_id\n\n        # Still ambiguous - return None to trigger clarification\n        return None\n\n    def clear_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear conversation state for a session.\"\"\"\n        self._focus.pop(session_id, None)\n        self._history.pop(session_id, None)\n        self._recent_topics.pop(session_id, None)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cleared conversation state for session {session_id}\")\n\n    def get_stats(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get conversation statistics for a session.\"\"\"\n        focus = self.get_focus(session_id)\n        history = self.get_history(session_id)\n\n        return {\n            \"has_focus\": focus is not acknowledged a result\n    FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN = \"follow_up_pattern\"  # Follow-up question patterns\n    SURFACE_SWITCH = \"surface_switch\"  # Audio-to-canvas switches\n    RESULT_REQUERY = switch\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Track result creation times for ack speed calculation\n        self._result_created_at: dict[str, int] = {}  # result_id -> created_at\n when a result was created for ack speed calculation.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        self._result_created_at[result_id] = now\n        logger.debug(f\"Tracked Optional[FeedbackSignal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track when a user acknowledged a result.\n\n        Generates an ack_speed signal.\n        \"\"\"\n        if result_id not in self._result_created_at:\n            logger.warning(f\"Result {result_id} not tracked for creation time\")\n            return None\n\n        created_at = self._result_created_at[result_id]\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        ack_delay_seconds = now - created_at\n\n        # Clean up old entries\n        del self._result_created_at[result_id]\n\n        # Determine signal quality\n        # Fast ack (< 10s) = positive signal\n        # Medium ack (10-60s) = neutral\n        # Slow ack (> 60s) = negative signal\n        # Very slow ack (> 300s) = strongly negative (may have been ignored)\n        if ack_delay_seconds < 10:\n            quality = \"positive\"\n        elif ack_delay_seconds < 60:\n            quality = \"neutral\"\n        elif ack_delay_seconds < 300:\n            quality = \"negative\"\n        else:\n            quality = \"strongly_negative\"\n\n        signal = FeedbackSignal(\n            signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n            signal_type=SignalType.ACK_SPEED,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            topic_id=None,  # We don't track topic for ack speed\n            timestamp=now,\n            data={\n                \"ack_delay_seconds\": ack_delay_seconds,\n                \"quality\": quality,\n                \"surface_type\": surface_type,\n            },\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n\n        await self._store_signal(signal)\n        logger.info(f\"Tracked ack_speed signal: {ack_delay_seconds}s ({quality})\")\n\n        return signal\n\n    async def track_utterance(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: Optional[str],\n        is_follow_up: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track an utterance for pattern detection.\n\n        Generates follow_up_pattern and topic_switch/continuation signals.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        # Store utterance\n        if session_id not in self._recent_utterances:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = []\n\n        self._recent_utterances[session_id].append({\n            \"utterance\": utterance,\n            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n            \"timestamp\": now,\n            \"is_follow_up\": is_follow_up,\n        })\n\n        # Keep only last 50 utterances\n        if len(self._recent_utterances[session_id]) > 50:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = self._recent_utterances[session_id][-50:]\n\n        # Check for follow-up pattern\n        if is_follow_up and topic_id:\n            await self._analyze_follow_up_pattern(session_id, utterance, topic_id, now)\n\n        # Check for topic switch vs continuation\n        await self._analyze_topic_switch(session_id, topic_id, now)\n\n    async def _analyze_follow_up_pattern(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        timestamp: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Analyze follow-up patterns to detect what was missing from previous result.\"\"\"\n        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n\n        # Look for the previous utterance on the same topic\n        prev_utterance = None\n        for u in reversed(utterances[:-1]):  # Exclude current utterance\n            if u[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id:\n                prev_utterance = u\n                break\n\n        if not prev_utterance:\n            return\n\n        # Analyze follow-up type\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n\n        follow_up_type = None\n        if any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"why\", \"how come\", \"reason\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"why_question\"  # Previous result lacked explanation\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"how long\", \"when\", \"time\"]):\n       follow_up_type = \"more_detail\"  # Previous result was insufficient\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"status\", \"current state\", \"what's happening\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"status_check\"  # User checking if state changed\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"fix\", \"solve\", \"resolve\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"action_request\"  # User wants to take action\n\n        if follow_up_type:\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"follow_up_type\": follow_up_type,\n                    \"utterance\": utterance,\n                    \"prev_utterance\": prev_utterance[\"utterance\"],\n                    \"time_since_prev\": timestamp - prev_utterance[\"timestamp\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked follow_up_pattern: {follow_up_type}\")\n\n    async def _analyze_topic_switch(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_id: Optional[str],\n        timestamp: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Analyze whether user switched topics or continued.\"\"\"\n        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n\n        if len(utterances) < 2:\n            return\n\n        # Compare with previous utterance\n        prev_utterance = utterances[-2]\n        prev_topic_id = prev_utterance.get(\"topic_id\")\n\n        if prev_topic_id == topic_id and topic_id is not None:\n            # Topic continuation\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.TOPIC_CONTINUATION,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"turn_count\": sum(1 for u in utterances if u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id),\n                    \"time_in_topic\": timestamp - min(\n                        u[\"timestamp\"] for u in utterances if u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id\n                    ) if any(u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id for u in utterances) else 0,\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n\n        elif prev_topic_id != topic_id and prev_topic_id and topic_id:\n            # Topic switch\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.TOPIC_SWITCH,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"from_topic_id\": prev_topic_id,\n                    \"to_topic_id\": topic_id,\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked topic_switch: {prev_topic_id} -> {topic_id}\")\n\n    async def track_surface_switch(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        from_surface: str,\n        to_surface: str,\n        pending_result_count: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track a surface switch event.\n\n        Generates surface_switch signal. Audio-to-canvas with pending results\n        indicates poor narration or complex data.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        if session_id not in self._surface_switches:\n            self._surface_switches[session_id] = []\n\n        switch_data = {\n            \"from_surface\": from_surface,\n            \"to_surface\": to_surface,\n            \"timestamp\": now,\n            \"pending_result_count\": pending_result_count,\n        }\n\n        self._surface_switches[session_id].append(switch_data)\n\n        # Keep only last 20 switches\n        if len(self._surface_switches[session_id]) > 20:\n            self._surface_switches[session_id] = self._surface_switches[session_id][-20:]\n\n        # If switching from audio to canvas with pending results, signal\n        if from_surface == \"audio\" and to_surface == \"canvas\" and pending_result_count > 0:\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.SURFACE_SWITCH,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=None,\n                timestamp=now,\n                data={\n                    \"from_surface\": from_surface,\n                    \"to_surface\": to_surface,\n                    \"pending_result_count\": pending_result_count,\n                    \"reason\": \"audio_to_canvas_with_pending\",\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked surface_switch: audio->canvas with {pending_result_count} pending results\")\n\n    async def track_requery(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        previous_result_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track a re-query (user asked same/similar question again).\n\n        Indicates previous result was inadequate.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        # Normalize utterance for comparison\n        normalized = self._normalize_utterance(utterance)\n\n        if normalized not in self._query_attempts:\n            self._query_attempts[normalized] = []\n\n        self._query_attempts[normalized].extend(previous_result_ids)\n\n        # If we've seen this query before, signal re-query\n        if len(self._query_attempts[normalized]) > len(previous_result_ids):\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.RESULT_REQUERY,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=previous_result_ids[-1] if previous_result_ids else None,\n                topic_id=None,\n                timestamp=now,\n                data={\n                    \"utterance\": utterance,\n                    \"normalized\": normalized,\n                    \"previous_result_ids\": previous_result_ids,\n                    \"attempt_count\": len(self._query_attempts[normalized]),\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked result_requery: attempt {len(self._query_attempts[normalized])}\")\n\n    async def track_result_skipped(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        time_until_skip: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track when a result was skipped (not acknowledged before surface disconnect).\n\n        Indicates low interest or poor delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        signal = FeedbackSignal(\n            signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n            signal_type=SignalType.RESULT_SKIPPED,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            topic_id=None,\n            timestamp=now,\n            data={\n                \"time_until_skip_seconds\": time_until_skip,\n            },\n        )\n\n        await self._store_signal(signal)\n        logger.info(f\"Tracked result_skipped: {result_id} after {time_until_skip}s\")\n\n    async def _store_signal(self, signal: FeedbackSignal) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Store a signal in the session store.\"\"\"\n        store = get_store()\n        await store.create_feedback_signal(\n            signal_id=signal.signal_id,\n            signal_type=signal.signal_type.value,\n            session_id=signal.session_id,\n            result_id=signal.result_id,\n            topic_id=signal.topic_id,\n            timestamp=signal.timestamp,\n            data=signal.data,\n        )\n\n    def _normalize_utterance(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize utterance for comparison.\"\"\"\n        return utterance.lower().strip()\n\n    def get_session_signals(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        signal_type: Optional[SignalType] = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get recent signals for a session (for background analysis).\"\"\"\n        # This would query the session store in production\n        # For now, return empty list\n        return []\n\n    def clear_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear tracking data for a session.\"\"\"\n        self._recent_utterances.pop(session_id, None)\n        self._surface_switches.pop(session_id, None)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cleared feedback tracking for session {session_id}\")\n\n\n# Global feedback tracker instance\n_feedback_tracker: Optional[ImplicitFeedbackTracker] = None\n\n\ndef get_feedback_tracker() -> ImplicitFeedbackTracker:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global feedback tracker instance.\"\"\"\n    global _feedback_tracker\n    if _feedback_tracker is None:\n        _feedback_tracker = ImplicitFeedbackTracker()\n    return _feedback_tracker\n","numLines":439,"startLine":1,"totalLines":439}}}

for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint (placeholder for Phase 1).\n\n    For now, returns a simple routing result.\n    In full implementation, this calls the intent router LLM.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Placeholder: return a simple intent\n    # Full implementation will call LLM-based router\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Create a placeholder intent\n    intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n        utterance_id=utterance_id or str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        project_slug=None,\n        intent_type=\"lookup\",  # Placeholder\n    )\n\n    # For now, create a placeholder result immediately\n    # In full implementation, results arrive from fetch+synthesize\n    result_id = await store.create_result(\n        intent_id=intent_id,\n        topic_id=None,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        summary=\"This is a placeholder result. The full router will be implemented in Phase 1.\",\n        data={\"status\": \"placeholder\", \"utterance\": utterance},\n        urgency=\"normal\"\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"intents\": [\n            {\n                \"id\": intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"lookup\",\n                \"status\": \"resolved\"\n            }\n        ],\n        \"results\": [\n            {\n                \"id\": result_id,\n                \"summary\": \"Placeholder result\"\n            }\n        ]\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a session.\n\n    Returns topics with staleness info for canvas rendering.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _topic_manager:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# require_approval: bool = True\n\n\nclass ApprovalRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n\n\nclass RollbackRequest(BaseModel):\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: str\n\n\nclass        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve response.message,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/reject\")\nasync def api_v1_reject_change(request: ApprovalRequest, reason: Optional[str]
